chapters.title: Expressing Doubt & Emotion
chapters.description: Master the present subjunctive to express desires, doubts, and emotions.

lessons.prompt: Which sentence uses the subjunctive correctly?
lessons.explanation: Verbs of hope ('esperar') trigger the subjunctive 'vengas'.

lessons.prompt: Dudo que él ___ la verdad.
lessons.explanation: 'Saber' in the present subjunctive is 'sepa'.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'It is important that we study'

chapters.title: Hypothetical Situations
chapters.description: Combine the conditional and past subjunctive for 'if' clauses.

lessons.prompt: What does 'Si tuviera dinero' mean?
lessons.explanation: This uses the imperfect subjunctive for an unlikely current situation.

lessons.prompt: Si yo fuera tú, no lo ___.
lessons.explanation: The conditional 'haría' (I would do) pairs with the past subjunctive.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'If we studied more, we would pass'
lessons.hint: Use imperfect subjunctive for the 'if' clause, conditional for the result.

chapters.title: Idioms & Colloquialisms
chapters.description: Sound like a native by using common regional phrases and idioms.

lessons.prompt: What does the idiom 'Tomar el pelo' literally translate to, and what does it mean?
options: Take the hair / To go bald
Drink the hair / To be confused
Take the hair / To pull someone's leg
Cut the hair / To start fresh
lessons.correctAnswer: Take the hair / To pull someone's leg
lessons.explanation: It means to joke or trick someone playfully.

lessons.prompt: No cuesta un ojo de la ___, es muy barato.
lessons.explanation: 'Costar un ojo de la cara' means to cost an arm and a leg.

lessons.prompt: Translate the idiom: 'It's a piece of cake'

chapters.title: Politics & Society
chapters.description: Discuss government, laws, and social issues.

lessons.prompt: What are 'Los derechos humanos'?
lessons.explanation: 'Derechos' translates to rights.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'The government passed a new law'
lessons.correctAnswer: El gobierno aprobó una nueva ley

lessons.prompt: Las ___ se llevarán a cabo en noviembre.
lessons.explanation: 'Elecciones' means elections.

chapters.title: Arts & Literature
chapters.description: Critique paintings, books, and cultural movements.

lessons.prompt: What does 'La obra maestra' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Obra' is work (of art), 'maestra' is master.

lessons.prompt: El ___ principal de la novela es fascinante.
lessons.explanation: 'Personaje' refers to a character in a story.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'It is a contemporary painting'

chapters.title: Science & Future
chapters.description: Discuss discoveries using the Future Perfect and Conditional Perfect.

lessons.prompt: Which of these is the Future Perfect tense?
lessons.explanation: 'Habré' is the future form of 'haber'.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'By 2050, we will have discovered it'
Yo ___ ido, pero no tuve tiempo.
lessons.explanation: 'Habría ido' (Conditional Perfect) means 'I would have gone'.

chapters.title: Debates & Opinions
chapters.description: Learn connectors to construct strong arguments and express viewpoints.

lessons.prompt: What does 'Sin embargo' mean?
lessons.explanation: It is a common contrast connector.

lessons.prompt: Por un lado es caro, pero por otro ___, es de alta calidad.
lessons.explanation: 'Por otro lado' means 'on the other hand'.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'From my point of view'

chapters.title: Business & Economics
chapters.description: Navigate corporate vocabulary and economic trends.

lessons.prompt: What are 'Las inversiones'?
lessons.explanation: 'Invertir' is the verb to invest.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'The company went bankrupt'
lessons.correctAnswer: La empresa se fue a la quiebra
lessons.hint: 'Ir a la quiebra' means to go bankrupt.

lessons.prompt: Hemos firmado un nuevo ___ comercial.
lessons.explanation: 'Acuerdo' means agreement or contract.

chapters.title: Cultural Nuances
chapters.description: Understand subtle cultural phrasing and courtesy rules.

lessons.prompt: What does it mean if someone says 'Buen provecho'?
lessons.explanation: Said before eating, similar to 'Bon appétit'.

lessons.prompt: Te lo agradezco de todo ___.
lessons.explanation: 'De todo corazón' means with all my heart.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Make yourself at home'

chapters.title: Abstract Concepts
chapters.description: Discuss philosophy, psychology, and theoretical ideas.

lessons.prompt: What does 'El libre albedrío' mean?
lessons.explanation: A common philosophical term for freedom of choice.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Happiness is subjective'
lessons.hint: Remember to use the feminine article for 'felicidad'.

lessons.prompt: El ___ del universo es un misterio.
lessons.explanation: 'Origen' means origin or beginning.
